stat_of_week: 92 - Hiroshima Radiation's pitching staff racked up 92 strikeouts across 88.2 innings in a single week, setting a new season record and demolishing the previous mark of 92 set by... themselves, this very week — the first time any team has reached that threshold all season
thriller_note: A dead-even tie saw the Skidmarks dominate the power categories with 11 HR while the Wombats countered with superior pitching efficiency and RBI production, leaving both teams frustrated and no closer to pulling away from the bottom half of the standings heading into crucial week 13 matchups.
key_storyline: Jacob Misiorowski's absolutely absurd 0.11 WHIP across 9.0 innings headlined a Riverside Roughnecks pitching performance that buried General Hospital 7–2, with Jackson Chourio (5 HR, 10 RBI) and Nick Kurtz (4 HR, 7 RBI) providing a power barrage that made it a rout from the jump. The Roughnecks' combination of elite pitching and thunderous offense is the most complete performance profile in the league right now, and their week 13 showdown with Hiroshima Radiation — owners of a brand-new season strikeout record — sets up as the marquee matchup of the season. Whoever wins that duel will stake a serious claim to being the class of the league entering the final stretch.
lucky_reason: The Dragons won 7–3 over BaseballPro77 despite being outpitched in strikeouts and posting pedestrian counting stats across the board, benefiting from a cumulative luck delta of +2.12 that remains one of the highest in the league and suggests their record significantly flatters their true performance level.
unlucky_reason: Despite setting a new season strikeout record with 92 Ks, throwing 88.2 innings, posting 4 quality starts, and winning their matchup 7–3, Hiroshima's cumulative luck delta sits at –1.08, meaning they have consistently produced elite results that their overall record has not fully rewarded across the season.
records_broken: Hiroshima Radiation set a new season record for team strikeouts in a single week with 92 K across 88.2 innings, surpassing the previous season high of 92 — notably, this is the first team to reach that threshold, making it a new benchmark rather than a tie with a prior mark; additionally, Albuquerque Bombers matched the season record for most RBI in a week with 40, tying North Correa's mark from week 8.
power_rankings: 1. Riverside Roughnecks | Misiorowski's 0.11 WHIP, Chourio's 5-HR/10-RBI explosion, and a 7–2 demolition of General Hospital make this the most complete team in the league; week 13 vs. Hiroshima is a true title-contender clash | up

2. Albuquerque Bombers | An 8–2 beatdown fueled by Buxton's 4 HR, Herrera's .607 OBP, and a balanced pitching line confirms this roster is elite top-to-bottom; they tied the season RBI record and face General Hospital in week 13 — a winnable tuneup | same

3. Hiroshima Radiation | Setting a new season strikeout record with 92 K while winning 7–3 is a statement week; Soderstrom (4 HR, 9 RBI), Gelof (8 R), and a three-man rotation that posted 4 QS make this the most dangerous pitching-forward team in the league | up

4. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood's 3-HR, 9-run, 3-SB monster week and Cristopher Sánchez's 13-K outing powered a 7–3 win; the luck index remains inflated but the talent is undeniable heading into a tough North Correa matchup | same

5. Sugar Land Skeeters | Losing 8–2 to the Bombers is an ugly result, but the offense still showed life with Muncy, Olson, and Tatis contributing; the rotation's 7.28 ERA is a genuine crisis that must be addressed before the Skidmarks matchup | down

6. North Correa | A 5–5 tie is a disappointing result for a roster led by Ohtani (.542 OBP, 3 HR) and a lineup loaded with on-base threats; zero quality starts all week is the glaring rot underneath, and week 13 vs. the Dragons is a brutal draw | same

7. BaseballPro77 | A 7–3 loss to the Dragons stings but Kirby, Suárez, and Witt Jr. showed flashes; the cumulative luck delta of +1.27 means the schedule has been a friend — a week 13 date with Wisconsin Rapids will reveal how real this team is | down

8. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Winning 5–4 over the Twins on the strength of NSVH (9), solid pitching, and surprising depth from Okamoto and Andrew Vaughn makes this week the most encouraging performance of their season; more on this team below | up

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Alec Burleson's 5 HR and 8 RBI was a genuine standout performance but a 5.22 ERA and a razor-thin 5–4 loss shows the rotation remains the team's Achilles heel; adding Pallante and Carpenter signals an effort to patch the holes | down

10. General Hospital | Getting steamrolled 7–2 by the Roughnecks despite decent pitching (3.72 ERA) exposes an offense that simply cannot generate enough runs — only 25 scored all week — and adding Kolek and Montgomery suggests a roster rethink is underway | same

11. Buttville Skidmarks | A 5–5 tie with the Wombats when you score 33 runs and go yard 11 times should be a win; zero stolen bases cost them two category wins and dropping Vientos without a quality replacement thins an already shallow bench | same

12. Benchwarmers | Sonny Gray's 1.50 ERA gem and Spencer Arrighetti's 14 Ks were genuinely excellent, but a flat offense (only 20 runs, .278 OBP) handed Hiroshima a 7–3 win; structural offensive weakness continues to cap this team's ceiling | same

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Paul Skenes delivered 17 K and 2 QS — a legitimately elite pitching performance — but the offense mustered only 8 HR and 21 RBI in a 5–5 tie; adding Taylor, Ruiz, and Jordan signals urgency but these are depth moves, not difference-makers | up

14. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell's 8 runs and Henderson's contributions weren't enough to push past a 5–5 tie, and with only 37 IP and 33 K from the pitching staff they remain dependent on opponents making mistakes; the –1.96 cumulative luck delta is the worst in the league | down
